title: Varnish Ban List Growth and Lurker Lag
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder, Splunk Connect for Syslog, or HTTP Event Collector; `props.conf`/`transforms.conf` for listed sourcetypes
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="varnish:stats"
| eval bans=tonumber(bans), done=tonumber(bans_completed)
| eval lag=bans-done
| where lag > 1000
| table _time, host, bans, done, lag
description: Ban lag leaves outdated objects live especially on busy news sites.
implementation: Investigate slow lurker or excessive `ban()` calls from publishers.
visualization: Time series for rates and latency, top/dedup for hotspots, single-value alerts on health thresholds.
grandmaExplanation: We watch varnish ban list growth and lurker lag and catch issues early, before they turn into outages for the people who rely on the network.
knownFalsePositives: Edge cases: multi-vendor mixes and ad hoc feeds can include lab traffic and partial visibility that mimics issues until scopes are defined. Tuning tip: match this to «Varnish Ban List Growth and Lurker Lag» and exclude change windows, scans, and lab VLANs you already expect.
